usage: Papers/Research archive page hero — scholarly knowledge, accumulated research, published findings
animal_metaphor: Raven — intelligence, prophecy, keeper of knowledge (Huginn and Muninn: thought and memory), scholarly tradition
prompt: A highly detailed 18th century natural history engraving of a common raven (Corvus corax) perched atop a neat stack of three leather-bound folio volumes, rendered in the precise copper-plate engraving style of John Gould or Thomas Bewick. The raven faces three-quarters right, its head slightly tilted downward as if reading, with its thick curved bill slightly open. Every feather is individually rendered with meticulous cross-hatching showing the iridescent sheen of the plumage through varying line density. The throat hackles are prominent and textured with fine parallel lines. The raven's intelligent eye is large, round, and piercing — the focal point. The stack of books beneath shows spine lettering suggested by tiny parallel dashes, and the topmost volume is slightly open with a few pages visible at the fore-edge. A single loose manuscript page with faintly suggested handwritten lines curls from under the books onto the surface below. The talons grip the book spine firmly, each toe articulated with anatomical precision. Clean white background with a thin ruled rectangular border. No color, no modern elements, no digital effects. Pure black ink copperplate engraving on cream paper. Museum print quality, 18th century scientific illustration.
